title: The Veil of Ignorance
description: A method for determining the morality of issues by imagining oneself behind a veil of ignorance.
definition: A thought experiment by John Rawls where parties choosing principles of justice are placed behind a 'veil of ignorance' that deprives them of all knowledge of their personal characteristics and social status. This ensures impartiality, as no one is advantaged or disadvantaged in the choice of principles by the outcome of natural chance or social circumstances. It leads to the selection of Rawls's two principles of justice.

proofs.description: Introduced in John Rawls' 'A Theory of Justice' (1971).
Widely used in political philosophy, ethics, and economics to model fairness.
